name: Ant.Button

unions.name: Button
unions.comment: Represents a button component

unions.name: ButtonSize
unions.comment: Determines the size of the button

unions.name: ButtonType
unions.comment: The type of the button

values.name: button
values.comment:
Create a Button component.

    button "Click Me!"
        |> toHtml

values.name: disabled
values.comment:
Make the button disabled. If you have a `onClick` event registered, it will not be fired.

    button "You can't click this"
        |> onClick Logout
        |> disabled True
        |> toHtml

values.name: onClick
values.comment:
Make your button emit messages. By default, clicking a button does nothing.

    button "submit"
        |> onClick FinalCheckoutFormSubmitted
        |> toHtml

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Turn your Button into Html msg

values.name: withType
values.comment:
Change the default type of the Button

    button "submit"
        |> withType Dashed
        |> toHtml

name: Ant.Divider

unions.name: Divider
unions.comment: A customizeable divider that is used to separate pieces of content.

unions.name: Line
unions.comment: The visual look of the divider

unions.name: Orientation
unions.comment: Orientation defines the positioning of the divider label.

unions.name: TextStyle
unions.comment: Defines the size and emphasis of the divider label.

unions.name: Type
unions.comment: Whether the divider is separating content vertically or horizontally.

values.name: divider
values.comment:
Create a Divider component.

    Divider.divider
        |> Divider.toHtml

values.name: withLine
values.comment: Defines the line type of the divider.

values.name: withOrientation
values.comment: Defines the positioning of the label. If your divider doesn't have a label, then this option is redundant.

values.name: withTextStyle
values.comment: Defines whether the divider titles will get rendered as Headings or as smaller text.

values.name: withType
values.comment:
Change the type of the divider. By default the type is `Horizontal`

    verticalDivider =
        Divider.divider
            |> withType Vertical
            |> toHtml

name: Ant.Icons
Ant.Layout
comment:
Primitives for creating typical page layouts

From: <https://ant.design/components/layout/>


# Introduction

Each of the primitives below creates a `LayoutTree` that represents that shape of your layout.

This module allows for 2-column and 3-column layouts.

This module defines a recursive LayoutTree data structure, which allows for layouts of large depth and complexity.

@docs LayoutTree


## Primitives

The following primitives take some content (as `Html msg`) and returns to you a `LayoutTree` that you can continue appending to

Example:

    sidebar =
        Layout.sidebar (componentMenu model.activeRoute)
            |> Layout.sidebarWidth 300
            |> Layout.sidebarToTree

    layout : LayoutTree Msg
    layout =
        Layout.layout2
            (Layout.header <| toUnstyled navBar)
            (Layout.layout2
                sidebar
                (Layout.layout2
                    (Layout.content <| toUnstyled componentPageShell)
                    (Layout.footer <| toUnstyled footer)
                )
            )

@docs header, content, footer, layout, layout2


## Customizeable Primitives

The following primitives do not return a `LayoutTree` immediately. Rather, they return an intermediary data structure that you can then customize further before converting it into a `LayoutTree` node that can be appended to your main `LayoutTree`

@docs Sidebar, sidebar, sidebarWidth, sidebarToTree


## Other

@docs toHtml

unions.name: LayoutTree
unions.comment: The various kinds of nodes represented by a LayoutTree

unions.name: Sidebar
unions.comment: A sidebar

values.name: content
values.comment:
Create a content node in your LayoutTree

This node will be rendered beneath a header (if a header is provided), but above the footer (if a footer is provided)

values.name: footer
values.comment:
Create a footer node in your LayoutTree

This node will be rendered beneath all other nodes in your LayoutTree, just as you would expect from a footer.

values.name: header
values.comment:
Create a header node in your LayoutTree

This node will be rendered horizontally at the top of your layout tree

values.name: layout
values.comment: Generate a three-column layout from three LayoutTree's (see example above)
values.type: Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg -> Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg -> Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg -> Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg

values.name: layout2
values.comment: Generate a two-column layout from two LayoutTree's (see example above)
values.type: Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg -> Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg -> Ant.Layout.LayoutTree msg

values.name: sidebar
values.comment:
Create a customizeable sidebar

In order to append the sidebar to your LayoutTree, you must call `sidebarToTree`

    sideBar innerNodes
        |> sidebarWidth 40
        |> sidebarToTree

values.name: sidebarToTree
values.comment: Convert a `Sidebar` into a `LayoutTree` node so that you may append it to a `LayoutTree`

values.name: sidebarWidth
values.comment: Customize the width (in pixels) of the sidebar

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Convert your layout tree into good old `Html msg`

name: Ant.Menu
comment:
Primitives for creating Menus.

This module comes with three big menu primitives:

  - Menu: The main container of your menu
  - SubMenu: A menu within your menu. SubMenu's are collapsable.
  - ItemGroup: A grouping of items with a label.

The overarching idea in this module is that you start with the inner items of your menu, constructing it piece by piece, until you have
all of the elements of the Menu. Once that is done, then you can create your Menu and turn it into `Html msg`.

A good example can be found in this project's [showcase](https://github.com/supermacro/elm-antd/blob/master/showcase/src/elm/Router.elm#L277).

@docs initMenuItem, selected, initMenu, initSubMenu, initItemGroup, pushItem, pushSubMenu, pushItemGroup, pushItemToSubMenu, pushItemGroupToSubMenu, pushSubMenuToSubMenu, pushItemToItemGroup, toHtml, Menu, SubMenu, ItemGroup, MenuMode

unions.name: ItemGroup
unions.comment: Represents a grouping of MenuItems

unions.name: Menu
unions.comment: Represents a Menu, its configuration and the Menu's content

unions.name: MenuMode
unions.comment: This type defines how the menu will be positioned

unions.name: SubMenu
unions.comment: Represents a sub-menu, and the submenu's associated configuration / state, as well as the menu's contents

values.name: initItemGroup
values.comment: Initialize a item group with a set of associated MenuItem's

values.name: initMenu
values.comment: Initialize a menu with no content. This is useful when constructing your menu using folds.

values.name: initMenuItem
values.comment: Given a `msg` and some contents, create a menu item

values.name: pushItem
values.comment: push a menu item to the end of the menu
values.type: Ant.Menu.MenuItem msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg

values.name: pushItemGroup
values.comment: push a item group to the end of the menu
values.type: Ant.Menu.ItemGroup msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg

values.name: pushItemGroupToSubMenu
values.comment: Push a Item Group to the end of the SubMenu
values.type: Ant.Menu.ItemGroup msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg

values.name: pushItemToItemGroup
values.comment: Add a MenuItem to the end of the ItemGroup
values.type: Ant.Menu.MenuItem msg -> Ant.Menu.ItemGroup msg -> Ant.Menu.ItemGroup msg

values.name: pushItemToSubMenu
values.comment: Push a MenuItem to the end of the SubMenu
values.type: Ant.Menu.MenuItem msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg

values.name: pushSubMenu
values.comment: push a SubMenu to the end of the menu
values.type: Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg -> Ant.Menu.Menu msg

values.name: pushSubMenuToSubMenu
values.comment: Push a SubMenu to the end of the SubMenu
values.type: Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg -> Ant.Menu.SubMenu msg

values.name: selected
values.comment: Mark the menu item as selected

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Turn your Menu into a `Html msg`

name: Ant.Space

unions.name: SpaceDirection
unions.comment: Direction of the layout (think flexbox direction)

unions.name: SpaceSize
unions.comment: The size of the space between elements

values.name: direction
values.comment:
Set the direction of your Space value\\

    space myElementList
        |> direction Horizontal

values.name: space
values.comment:
Create a Space value with default values

    space [ myEl, myOtherEl, mySuperCoolEl ]

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Convert your Space into a `Html msg`

name: Ant.Tooltip
comment:
Tooltips for your enjoyment!

@docs tooltip, Tooltip, toHtml, withPosition, TooltipPosition

unions.name: Tooltip
unions.comment: Represents a customizeable tooltip

unions.name: TooltipPosition
unions.comment: Defines the positioning of the Tooltip

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Convert the Tooltip into a `Html msg`

values.name: tooltip
values.comment:
Create a Tooltip

    tooltip "helpful message"
        |> toHtml

values.name: withPosition
values.comment:
Change the default positioning of the tooltip

    tooltip "on the right"
        |> withPosition Right
        |> toHtml

name: Ant.Typography
comment:
Typography components for Elm Antd

@docs title, Level, level, toHtml

unions.name: Level
unions.comment: Level of your headings

values.name: level
values.comment:
Change the heading level of your `Title` component

    title "Elm is cool"
        |> level H3
        |> toHtml

values.name: title
values.comment:
Create a `Title` / header component

By default, the `Title` produced is a `H1`

values.name: paragraph
values.comment: Evenly space out a set of children

name: Ant.Typography.Text
comment:
Create decorated text values

@docs TextType, Text, text, code, keyboard, textType, strong, disabled, underlined, lineThrough, highlighted, toHtml, listToHtml

unions.name: Text
unions.comment: A text value

unions.name: TextType
unions.comment: What kind of text is it?

values.name: code
values.comment:
Turn your Text value into a styled in-line code block text "

I LOVE CATS

" |> code |> toHtml

values.name: disabled
values.comment:
Set your Text element as disabled (the cursor icon changes)

    text "can't touch this'"
        |> disabled True
        |> toHtml

values.name: highlighted
values.comment:
Style the Text as highlighted

    text "super important"
        |> highlighted True
        |> toHtml

values.name: keyboard
values.comment:
Turn your Text value into a styled button-looking block (in-line)

    text "CMD"
        |> keyboard
        |> toHtml

values.name: lineThrough
values.comment:
Set a striked line through your Text element

    text "forget about this"
        |> lineThrough True
        |> toHtml

values.name: listToHtml
values.comment: Render a list of Text elements into a span

values.name: strong
values.comment:
Set your Text element as bold

    text "look at me"
        |> strong
        |> toHtml

values.name: text
values.comment:
Create a text value

By default the text looks just like any regular text. To decorate it, you need to apply one or more of the below options.

text "hello, world!"
|> textType Warning
|> underlined True
|> strong
|> toHtml

values.name: textType
values.comment:
Change the text's type
text "Elm"
|> textType Secondary
|> toHtml

values.name: toHtml
values.comment: Render your Text node into plain old `Html msg`

values.name: underlined
values.comment:
Add an underline to your text

    text "this is important"
        |> underlined True
        |> toHtml
